name: HMCS Red Deer (J 255)
events: 12 Jan 1942 The British merchant Cyclops is torpedoed and sunk by German U-boat U-123 about 125 miles southeast of Cape Sable, Nova Scotia, Canada in position 41°51'N, 63°48'W. HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. A. Moorhouse, RCNR) later picks up 95 survivors.

9 Aug 1944 HMS L 27 (Lt. J.N. Elliott, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Trillium (T/Lt. K.E. Meredith, RCNVR), HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR), HMCS St. Lambert (T/Lt. R.C. Hayden, RCNVR) and HMCS Kentville (T/Lt. F.G. Rainsford, RCNVR). (1)
10 Aug 1944 HMS L 27 (Lt. J.N. Elliott, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Trillium (T/Lt. K.E. Meredith, RCNVR) and HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (1)
12 Aug 1944 HMS L 27 (Lt. J.N. Elliott, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Trillium (T/Lt. K.E. Meredith, RCNVR) and HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (1)

14 Aug 1944 HMS L 27 (Lt. J.N. Elliott, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Bermuda with HMCS Nene (T/A/Lt.Cdr. E.R. Shaw, RCNR), HMCS Asbestos (T/Lt. J. Cuthbert, RCNR), HMCS Barrie (T/Lt. W.D. Stokvis, RCNVR) and HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (1)

2 Sep 1944 Around 1000 O/2, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed St.Johns for Argentia, Newfoundland, Canada. They were escorted by the minesweeper HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (2)
3 Sep 1944 Around 1600 OP/3, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at Argentia from St. John's.
On arrival A/S exercises were carried out with their escort, HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
